articles.title: 83% of Ivanti EPMM Exploits Linked to Single IP on Bulletproof Hosting Infrastructure
articles.summary: A significant chunk of the exploitation attempts targeting a newly disclosed security flaw in Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) can be traced back to a single IP address on bulletproof hosting infrastructure offered by PROSPERO.  Threat intelligence firm GreyNoise said  it recorded 417 exploitation sessions from 8 unique source IP addresses between February 1 and 9, 2026. An estimated 346 exploitation sessions have originated from 193.24.123[.]42, accounting for 83% of all attempts.  The malicious activity is designed to exploit CVE-2026-1281  (CVSS scores: 9.8), one of the two critical security vulnerabilities in EPMM, along with CVE-2026-1340  that could be exploited by an attacker to achieve unauthenticated remote code execution. Late last month, Ivanti acknowledged it's aware of a "very limited number of customers" who were impacted following the zero-day exploitation of the issues.   Since then, multiple European agencies, including the Netherlands' Dutch Da...
articles.title_fr: 83 % des exploitations d'Ivanti EPMM liées à une seule adresse IP sur une infrastructure d'hébergement "bulletproof"
articles.summary_fr: Une grande partie des tentatives d'exploitation d'une nouvelle faille de sécurité dans Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) proviennent d'une seule adresse IP (193.24.123[.]42) hébergée sur l'infrastructure "bulletproof" de PROSPERO. Cette vulnérabilité critique, CVE-2026-1281, ainsi que CVE-2026-1340, permet l'exécution de code à distance non authentifiée. Ivanti a confirmé être au courant d'un nombre très limité de clients impactés par l'exploitation de ces failles zero-day.

articles.title: Apple Fixes Exploited Zero-Day Affecting iOS, macOS, and Apple Devices
articles.summary: Apple on Wednesday released  iOS, iPadOS, macOS Tahoe, tvOS, watchOS, and visionOS updates to address a zero-day flaw that it said has been exploited in sophisticated cyber attacks.  The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20700  (CVSS score: N/A), has been described as a memory corruption issue in dyld, Apple's Dynamic Link Editor. Successful exploitation of the vulnerability could allow an attacker with memory write capability to execute arbitrary code on susceptible devices. Google Threat Analysis Group (TAG) has been credited with discovering and reporting the bug.  "Apple is aware of a report that this issue may have been exploited in an extremely sophisticated attack against specific targeted individuals on versions of iOS before iOS 26," the company said in an advisory. "CVE-2025-14174 and CVE-2025-43529 were also issued in response to this report."   It's worth noting that both CVE-2025-14174 and CVE-2025-43529 were addressed  by Cupertino in Decembe...
articles.title_fr: Apple corrige une faille zero-day exploitée affectant iOS, macOS et d'autres appareils Apple
articles.summary_fr: Apple a publié des mises à jour pour iOS, iPadOS, macOS Tahoe et d'autres systèmes afin de corriger une faille zero-day (CVE-2026-20700) activement exploitée dans des cyberattaques sophistiquées. Cette vulnérabilité de corruption de mémoire dans dyld pourrait permettre à un attaquant d'exécuter du code arbitraire sur les appareils vulnérables. Apple a indiqué avoir connaissance de son exploitation ciblée contre des individus spécifiques sur des versions antérieures d'iOS.

articles.title: First Malicious Outlook Add-In Found Stealing 4,000+ Microsoft Credentials
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered what they said is the first known malicious Microsoft Outlook add-in detected in the wild.  In this unusual supply chain attack detailed  by Koi Security, an unknown attacker claimed the domain associated with a now-abandoned legitimate add-in to serve a fake Microsoft login page, stealing over 4,000 credentials in the process. The activity has been codenamed AgreeToSteal  by the cybersecurity company.  The Outlook add-in in question is AgreeTo , which is advertised by its developer as a way for users to connect different calendars in a single place and share their availability through email. The add-in was last updated in December 2022.  Idan Dardikman, co-founder and CTO of Koi, told The Hacker News that the incident represents a broadening of supply chain attack vectors.  "This is the same class of attack we've seen in browser extensions, npm packages, and IDE plugins: a trusted distribution channel where the content can change aft...
articles.title_fr: Premier complément Outlook malveillant découvert, dérobant plus de 4 000 identifiants Microsoft
articles.summary_fr: Des chercheurs en cybersécurité ont découvert le premier complément Microsoft Outlook malveillant connu, baptisé "AgreeToSteal", détecté en circulation. Dans cette attaque de chaîne d'approvisionnement, un attaquant a revendiqué le domaine d'un complément légitime abandonné pour servir une fausse page de connexion Microsoft, dérobant ainsi plus de 4 000 identifiants. Cet incident, qui exploite un canal de distribution de confiance, représente un élargissement des vecteurs d'attaque de la chaîne d'approvisionnement.
